narrative:
We have had two days with considerable cloudiness and cooler-than-average temperatures. After a cool start earlier, temperatures are warming up this afternoon. Tonight is notably milder than the last two nights. Warmer and more humid air continues to overtake the region Thursday, producing scattered afternoon showers and thunderstorms. A front passes through early Friday; it keeps the potential for widely scattered showers and thunderstorms in play Thursday night and Friday morning. Friday afternoon brings breaks in the clouds, a breezy NW wind, and cooler temperatures.

We now have the first named tropical storm of the year, Arthur, along the Texas coast. It is weak and messy. Arthur will track into Louisiana tonight, and will not survive the bayou as a tropical system. Its remnant low moves across the Southeast but far enough to our south to have no direct impact on our weather. But one indirect effect could be robbing energy from storms here Thursday night and early Friday.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Pre-Publication Sale is Underway!

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is being printed now and will be available around July 1. Until then, the Pre-Publication Sale is underway with the best price we will offer all year. Get your 2027 calendars at 26% off when you buy 2 or more. As in previous years, we have scheduled special Pickup times around NW NC to save you the cost of shipping. Click here for pickup times and locations.

It's the pre-summer solstice edition of the Monday Message, and we talk El Niño, as well our our next rainfall opporturnites conming up in the Thursday-Friday time frame.

In addition to our 15 winning photographs in the Blue Ridge Photo Contest, check out the 36 "Cameo Photo Winners" on the Photo Contest page .

Modest Warming Today

Last Updated Wed June 17 at 4:20PM by Alan Simmons

We have had two days with considerable cloudiness and cooler-than-average temperatures. After a cool start earlier, temperatures are warming up this afternoon. Tonight is notably milder than the last two nights. Warmer and more humid air continues to overtake the region Thursday, producing scattered afternoon showers and thunderstorms. A front passes through early Friday; it keeps the potential for widely scattered showers and thunderstorms in play Thursday night and Friday morning. Friday afternoon brings breaks in the clouds, a breezy NW wind, and cooler temperatures.

We now have the first named tropical storm of the year, Arthur, along the Texas coast. It is weak and messy. Arthur will track into Louisiana tonight, and will not survive the bayou as a tropical system. Its remnant low moves across the Southeast but far enough to our south to have no direct impact on our weather. But one indirect effect could be robbing energy from storms here Thursday night and early Friday.

Forecast Discussion

The ghost of the front that passed through Sunday night is dissipating over North Carolina, allowing warmer, moist air to return from the southwest. We have a warmer afternoon after our cool start earlier.

I will not repeat the paragraph about Tropical Storm Arthur from the Introduction, but we get an influx of moisture from the south thanks to that low and a front approaching from the northwest. Expect scattered showers and thunderstorms Thursday with even warmer temperatures. Showers and thundershowers remain in play Thursday night and Friday morning until the front passes to our south. We can expect some sun Friday afternoon with breezy conditions and cooler temperatures.

Saturday has a cool start and a pleasant afternoon with plenty of sun. High pressure moves quickly across the region, and a dry front moves through Saturday night with no noticeable effect.

The next significant front arrives early Tuesday morning. By late Sunday, we are under the influence of high pressure in the Gulf with a return flow from the SW already developing. Widely scattered showers and thundershowers are possible Sunday afternoon through Monday daytime. We'll have plenty of dry breaks in there. Tuesday looks somewhat cooler and dry with the front moving to our south.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for June 15, 2026

It's the pre-summer solstice edition of the Monday Message, available for subscribers. El Niño has officially arrived, and will have significant implications for our weather heading into fall and winter.

Jun 15, 2026
El Niño Has Officially Arrived

El Niño has officially arrived, as described in the latest Climate Prediction Center ENSO discussion.

This will have significant implications on fall and winter temperature and precipitation in the Southeastern United States. While our region continues to experience significant drought conditions, above-normal precipitation will ultimately make a comeback during fall and winter.

Jun 14, 2026
Monday Message for June 8, 2026

This week's Monday Message highlights a week of hit-or-miss showers and thundershowers that will be returning to the region in typical June fashion. Also, we discuss the movie "Pressure", in theaters now.

Jun 08, 2026
